summary: Monster Other One, He's Gone and a tight Philo stomp drive the night
blurb: The show delivers an expansive second‑set jam marathon highlighted by a monster The Other One and a scorching He's Gone. Phil’s bass “stomp” anchors the flow, while Jerry shreds through the extended improvisations. Overall it’s a high‑energy performance that stands out in the 1972 run.
review: Milwaukee Performing Arts Center, Oct 24 1972 – The Dead were in top form, rolling from the first set into a second‑set that feels like one continuous adventure. After a solid opener, the band launches into Truckin' and immediately dives into a thunderous drum break before bursting into an unforgettable The Other One jam that stretches and morphs with Jerry’s lead guitar shredding and Phil’s legendary bass stomp. He’s Gone follows, scorching through its lyrical climax and feeding back into another round of The Other One, cementing the night as one of the era’s most adventurous outings. The Miller soundboard capture (gd1972‑10‑24.sbd.miller.106018.flac16) renders the performance in near‑perfect clarity, letting listeners hear every nuance of the band’s tight interplay and high‑energy flow.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings describe the show as positive, praising a monster The Other One, scorching He's Gone, tight Truckin' jam and Phil's bass stomp. Only one recording is mixed (audience tape) but provides no negative performance criticism. Consistent high praise across multiple sources justifies a strong positive sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Near‑perfect Miller soundboard mix; excellent clarity, balance and captures the full energy of the performance.

key_highlights: Monster The Other One jam spanning both halves of the second set
Scorching He's Gone that rides the Philo bass stomp
Tight Truckin' leading into the improvisational flow
Phil Lesh’s gorgeous, central bass work throughout

band_performance.Jerry: Sharp, shredding lead work that drives the extended jams, with tone highlighted by the superb mix.
band_performance.Phil: Gorgeous bass performance; the famed Philo stomp anchors the set and he delivers an almost perfect solo bass jam.
band_performance.Bob: Solid rhythm guitar and vocal contributions that blend smoothly into the overall sound.
band_performance.Billy: Provides a strong, steady foundation; his drumming underpins the expansive improvisations.
